name: 5 Star Car Title Loans
description_short: 5 Star Car Title Loans offers secured auto title loans from $100–$50,000 with same-day funding, no credit check required, and borrowers keep their vehicle.
description_long:
5 Star Car Title Loans™ is a licensed consumer lender headquartered in San Bernardino, CA, that has been operating since 2012. Over that period the company reports funding more than 255,000 loans across customers in approximately 18 states, including California, Florida, Indiana, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Ohio, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, and Washington. It positions itself as a direct, regulated alternative to payday lenders for borrowers who own a vehicle with equity.

The company's primary product is a vehicle title loan: the borrower uses their car, motorcycle, or commercial vehicle title as collateral, receives a lump-sum cash advance ranging from $100 to $50,000, and continues driving the vehicle while making payments. A separate installment loan product is also offered with fixed monthly payments over repayment periods of up to 24 months for borrowers covering larger planned expenses. A same-day loan option targets urgent needs such as overdue rent, utility bills, or car repairs, with funds disbursed on the day of approval for qualified applicants.

Several features differentiate 5 Star from typical storefront title lenders. The company emphasizes no prepayment penalties, allowing borrowers to pay off early without extra cost. Applications can be completed online or in person, and the stated approval decision time is minutes. Loan availability does not require good credit—the underwriting is primarily asset-based—which broadens access for thin-file and subprime borrowers. The lender is licensed and state-regulated rather than operating through tribal or offshore structures, and it publishes upfront terms with no hidden fees per its own disclosures.

Honestly assessed, 5 Star Car Title Loans fills a real gap for vehicle owners facing a cash emergency who cannot qualify for conventional credit. However, title loans as a product class typically carry annual percentage rates well in excess of 100%, which the website does not disclose publicly—borrowers should request the full APR in writing before signing. The collateral arrangement means a missed payment could result in vehicle repossession. The review base (11 reviews, 4.7 stars) is very small relative to the claimed 255,000 loans funded, limiting the reliability of third-party sentiment data. Loan availability and terms also vary significantly by state, so consumers outside the listed service area cannot use this lender at all.
pros: Borrower keeps and drives the vehicle throughout the loan term
Same-day funding available for qualified applicants
No prepayment penalties — pay off early without extra fees
Licensed and regulated lender, not a tribal or offshore structure
Asset-based underwriting — poor or no credit history accepted
Online or in-person application with stated approval in minutes
Operates since 2012 with 255,000+ loans funded across ~18 states
cons: APR not disclosed on the website — title loans typically carry triple-digit rates
Vehicle can be repossessed if the borrower defaults on payments
Only available in approximately 18 states; many US residents are ineligible
Only 11 public reviews despite 14 years of operation, limiting third-party validation
Loan terms and amounts vary by state, meaning advertised maximums may not apply locally
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need emergency cash quickly
Borrowers who need same-day funding for urgent bills, rent, or repairs
People with vehicle equity but no access to personal loans or credit cards
Borrowers in the ~18 served states who want a licensed, regulated title lender
diagnosis: 5 Star Car Title Loans is best suited for vehicle owners who have an urgent cash need, cannot qualify for mainstream credit, and are comfortable using their car title as collateral. The main caveat is that title loan APRs are rarely competitive — borrowers should obtain the full APR disclosure before committing, and should treat this as a last-resort option given the repossession risk on a vehicle they may depend on.
services: In-person loan application at branch locations
